title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: SIMD source files use SIMDe types functions, backend names refer native target-specific staged kernel objects. configuration, optional backend accepted compiler flag SIMDe header together define expected native SIMDe macro. example, AVX2 probe compiles -mavx2, includes <simde/x86/avx2.h>, requires SIMDE_X86_AVX2_NATIVE. AVX-512 similarly requires native F, BW, VL macros. flags make compiler define target macros __AVX2__ __AVX512BW__. SIMDe maps SIMDE_ARCH_* SIMDE_*_NATIVE; SIMDe function bodies use native intrinsics macros fall back native macro absent. generated src/Makevars links staged objects package shared library dispatcher, CPU feature detection, R API compiled R’s ordinary src/Makevars path. installed diagnostics report backends passed SIMDe-native compile probe:

title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: following benchmark evaluated article built. uses small input package checks remain fast, still exercises -process backend switching public API. "auto" selects best backend compiled supported intersection. current ranking : Architecture guards mean x86 systems normally consider x86 backends ARM systems normally consider NEON. Scalar always available.
code: if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {   bench_x <- rep(as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 3, 0, 255, 7, 0)), length.out = 2^20)    bench <- bench::mark(     scalar = {       simd_set_backend("scalar")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     auto = {       simd_set_backend("auto")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     iterations = 5,     check = TRUE   )    simd_set_backend("auto")   bench[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "n_itr")] } #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   expression   median `itr/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm>     <dbl> #> 1 scalar        471µs     2114. #> 2 auto         55.6µs    18691. avx512 > avx2 > sse41 > sse2 > neon > scalar

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: downstream package root: pkg omitted, package name read DESCRIPTION. prefix omitted, lowercase C identifier prefix derived package name. helper performs important substitutions, including @useDynLib, R_init_<Package>(), rsd_, RSD_, RC_ symbols. copied scaffold includes:
code: RsimdDispatch::use_simd_dispatch(pkg = "MyPackage", prefix = "mypkg") configure configure.win cleanup tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh tools/kernels/kernel_scalar.c tools/kernels/kernel_sse2.c tools/kernels/kernel_sse41.c tools/kernels/kernel_avx2.c tools/kernels/kernel_avx512.c tools/kernels/kernel_neon.c src/Makevars.in src/Makevars.win.in src/cpu_features.c src/simd_dispatch.c

text: demo kernel shape: real package, replace demo kernel signature keep structure: put -mavx2, -mavx512*, -march=native global package flags. configure helper keeps ISA flags local optional staged kernel objects, dispatcher remains safe baseline CPUs.

what: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Pure-C runtime SIMD dispatch templates R packages. Stage scalar, SSE, AVX, AVX-512, NEON kernel objects configuration; link one shared library switch among compiled CPU-supported implementations runtime without unsafe overrides.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: GitHub:

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Copy C dispatch scaffold package root: helper updates DESCRIPTION LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch, writes scaffold files, substitutes package-specific registration C symbol prefixes. add runtime dependency RsimdDispatch. Replace demo count_nonzero() kernels tools/kernels/ scalar/SSE/AVX/NEON kernels. generated configure script probes compiler support, stages selected kernel objects src/rsd-kernels/, leaves src/Makevars link objects baseline R API, CPU detection, dispatcher. See package vignettes downstream-package workflow dispatch semantics.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: simd_set_backend() rejects uncompiled CPU-unsupported backends. unsafe override.
code: library(RsimdDispatch)  x <- as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 0, 255))  count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_backend() #> [1] "scalar" simd_set_backend("avx2") simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("auto") simd_info()[c("compiled_backends", "cpu_supported_backends", "available_backends", "simde_native_backends")] #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #>  #> $cpu_supported_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   #>  #> $simde_native_backends #> [1] "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" simde_info()[c("version", "commit")] #> $version #> [1] "0.8.4" #>  #> $commit #> [1] "f3e8262173b7089db9a9d57a9ecef8dd07ad9c97" simd_dispatch_template_path() #> [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/site-library/RsimdDispatch/templates/dispatch-c"

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Switching backends -process variants linked one R shared library selected guarded function pointers.
code: count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 simd_info()[c("selected_backend", "available_backends")] #> $selected_backend #> [1] "avx2" #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") scalar_count <- count_nonzero(x)  simd_set_backend("avx2") avx2_count <- count_nonzero(x)  simd_set_backend("auto") data.frame(   scalar = scalar_count,   avx2 = avx2_count,   selected_after_auto = simd_backend() ) #>   scalar avx2 selected_after_auto #> 1      3    3                avx2

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
code: set.seed(1) x <- as.raw(sample.int(256L, 50 * 1024 * 1024, replace = TRUE) - 1L)  mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     count_nonzero(x)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     count_nonzero(x)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(mark$median) * 1000,   mb_per_second = length(x) * as.numeric(mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- bench$mb_per_second / bench$mb_per_second[bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(bench, digits = 3)

RsimdDispatch 0.1.1 (2026-05-28)
text: Stage scalar optional SIMD kernel objects configure, link generated src/Makevars baseline R API, CPU feature detection, dispatcher code. Move demo kernels src/ tools/kernels/ copied templates keep build-time kernel sources separate ordinary R package src/ files. Reword DESCRIPTION avoid incoming spell-check notes backend acronym names keeping detailed backend names user-facing documentation.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Provides working runtime SIMD dispatch example R packages, using one shared library scalar, SSE2, SSE4.1, AVX2, AVX-512, NEON kernel objects supported compiler CPU. Exports count_nonzero(), simd_set_backend(), simd_backend(), simd_info() demonstrate safe -process backend selection diagnostics. Adds use_simd_dispatch() simd_dispatch_template_path() package authors want copy adapt C dispatch scaffold. Vendors header-SIMDe library downstream packages LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch. Adds simde_info() SIMDe fields simd_info() report vendored SIMDe version pinned upstream commit.
